methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
The Exploration Company, founded by Hélène Huby, is a Series B space and aerospace venture that has raised $160M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers across the sector, with direct same-stage comparables including Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $160M total raised proxy, yielding $304M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $400M, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B, reflecting material dispersion in the peer set. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied as the target's revenue is not on file, limiting the utility of the sector's 49.8x P/S median.

The triangulated range is $258M to $460M, with a midpoint of $352M. This 79% spread reflects the divergence between stage-based progression and peer multiples, and highlights two key uncertainties: the wide dispersion among same-stage comps (4.7x from min to max), and the absence of revenue visibility to calibrate against public market multiples. The valuation is most defensible in the $320M–$400M band, anchored to peer medians.
